overlaps.description: why: the GitHub rejection cascade extends the same feature-level reject handler and cleanup payload introduced by I1; impact: implementing remote close and marker behavior first would either duplicate subtree reject wiring or bind to unstable local payloads; action: land the local reject command and cleanup contract before adding GitHub cascade and regression coverage.

why: I2 changes lint and scaffold enforcement for the simplified feature-block structure that I1 defines canonically; impact: runtime enforcement could drift again if scaffolds are updated before the protocol-owned storage contract is fixed; action: finalize the protocol and storage-only FEATURE_PLANS contract before changing lint and plan-init behavior.

why: I3 migrates stored plans and adds consistency checks against the protocol contract declared in I1; impact: migration and regression fixtures would encode the wrong target structure if protocol ownership is not settled first; action: define the canonical protocol contract before rewriting FEATURE_PLANS content and tests.

why: I3 must migrate stored plans and fixture expectations to the exact runtime lint/scaffold behavior produced by I2; impact: migrating plans before the new enforcement lands would create temporary shapes that still fail plan-lint or require a second migration pass; action: update runtime enforcement first, then normalize FEATURE_PLANS and add regression coverage.

why: I2 changes apply-overlaps validation and full-block write semantics inside the same command handlers whose workflow responsibilities are defined in I1; impact: implementing runtime validation first would hard-code behavior before the full-snapshot contract and CLI responsibility split are finalized; action: lock the workflow contract before changing apply semantics.

why: I3 rewrites overlap procedures and regression coverage around the workflow contract declared in I1; impact: helper and doc alignment would drift if the full-snapshot responsibility model is not defined first; action: define the canonical overlap workflow before updating docs, scope reads, and regression guidance.

why: I3 aligns read/build helpers and tests with the exact validation and apply behavior implemented in I2; impact: workflow docs and regression fixtures would encode stale semantics if helper alignment lands before final apply rules are implemented; action: finish full-block validation and write behavior before updating overlap workflows and coverage.
